Due May 12 4/20 BCE: RFP received. Working on it. Due end of April 2/19 BCE: Awaiting RFP 2/2 BCE: Won't be able to attend 1/30 BCE: Invited to Rodeo 1/23 BCE: RFP should be sent out in the next 60 days 1/8/26 BCE: Reaching out next week to ask about RFP 12/26 BCE: Awaiting RFP 10/31 BCE: Closed RFQ. hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are attached, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. There is no rep-captured buyer rationale behind the 'Important' tag.
• The October 21 demo (63 minutes) with Erica Doucet-Maye, Leslie Crow, Scarlett Naquin and other BakerRipley stakeholders produced action items centered on Zycus-versus-Coupa differentiator decks and P2P invoicing, integrations and reporting deep dives. Merlin, intake-AI and agentic capabilities did not surface from the buyer.
• The March 17 follow-up was unrecorded but included Coupa, Conga and Ivalua reps, a competitive bake-off on classical P2P/SIM criteria. With Coupa displacement as the dominant theme and no buyer-voiced AI signals, this lands at Curious.

north_star_verdict.headline: BakerRipley cancelled RFP 26-03 on 20 May and is reissuing it with redrawn scope; Erica Doucet-Maye confirmed the new RFP on 1 Jul, so the deal is alive but reset. We are re-competing against Coupa, Conga and Ivalua with no engaged sponsor, a neutral buyer, and a Negative AI read.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 101 (15 Oct 2026 from 6 Jul 2026). Created 26 Aug 2025, ~314 days old, in Formal Evaluation throughout. Close date has slipped four times (Mar to Apr to Jun to Jul to 15 Oct). Amount cut $185k to $150k on 1 May. Last Salesforce activity 1 Jul 2026 (5 days ago, a rep-initiated email that Erica Doucet-Maye replied to, confirming a reissued RFP). Last buyer CALL 21 Oct 2025 (8+ months ago). RFP cancelled 20 May, reissue pending. Reissue plus submission, evaluation, shortlist, commercials, negotiation and legal is a minimum 120-150 days on aggressive enterprise timelines, so the 15 Oct date is optimistic unless the RFP republishes immediately. Verdict improving from prior Off Track to Slowing because the buyer re-engaged 1 Jul, but momentum is thin and the process has restarted.
evidence: Erica Doucet-Maye 1 Jul 2026: 'Yes, I can send you the RFP at that time. You can also find the procurement here Become a Vendor - BakerRipley' (SF Task/Outreach email 1 Jul 2026)
Next Step 20 May 2026: 'Scope is being changed, adding more functionality. BR Team is cancelling RFP and going back to drawing board' (SF Next_Step__c)
Amount cut 185000 to 150000 on 1 May 2026 (SF OpportunityFieldHistory)
Close date pushed 9 Jul to 15 Oct 2026 on 8 May 2026 (SF OpportunityFieldHistory)
17 Mar 2026 vendor's meeting for RFP 26-03 with Coupa, Conga and Ivalua reps present (SF event 17 Mar 2026 + AIS_Why)
Last buyer call 21 Oct 2025 demo, 8+ months ago (Avoma)

metrics.narrative: BakerRipley articulated clear operational pain on the 21 Oct 2025 demo but never quantified it. Erica Doucet-Maye (Procurement Specialist) framed her mandate as 'ensuring that we're helping provide the best value for cost savings for the agency', and pain was named across procurement (manual, email-based sourcing with no central RFI/RFP tool), contracts (manual W-9/TIN/SAM vendor validation, per Erica's question about storing verifiable vendor info), CLM (auto-renewal risk without utilization tracking), and AP (manual invoice-mailbox triage). No cost, headcount, cycle-time or error-rate baseline was stated by the buyer; the only figures on the call were rep-stated (Anna tail-spend '6-8% direct savings', Devika). Quantifying the value case with BakerRipley finance is a ranked move and is required before any forecast lift.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Erica Doucet-Maye on cost-savings mandate
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Devika rep-stated 6-8% tail-spend savings
SF Value_to_Customer__c / Compelling_Event__c unfilled

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged, but the evidence points to Muhammadou Drammeh, Controller / Sr Director, as the budget owner: on the 21 Oct 2025 demo he introduced himself as 'controller of the agency', chaired the stakeholder roll-call ('So I'll be calling names'), and steered scope questions ('Let the procurement team handle that'). As agency Controller he is the natural approver of a $150k P2P/CLM spend, so EB is inferred-mapped rather than a true gap. However he has not engaged commercially since Oct 2025 and no pricing, budget or approval-authority conversation has occurred. Converting Drammeh from demo attendee to an active sponsor who confirms budget for the redrawn scope is the top qualification gap.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Muhammadou Drammeh, 'controller of the agency', chaired roll-call
OpportunityContactRole: Muhammadou Drammeh, Controller / Sr Director
SF Who_will_approve_budget__c / Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c unfilled

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is running as a formal public RFP (RFP 26-03 P2P and Contract Management System) rather than a Zycus-shaped criteria list, so the criteria are procurement-defined and partly opaque to us. From the 21 Oct 2025 demo the buyer surfaced their real weighting: Leslie Crow (Assistant General Counsel) explicitly asked us to differentiate versus Coupa ('if you could highlight any differences... reasons why your system is better than Coupa'), signaling CLM strength and AI depth matter; Erica pressed on vendor-master compliance (W-9/TIN/SAM), and Erica and Muhammadou closed by asking for a P2P-invoice deep dive and a reporting focus. The scope has since been redrawn (20 May), so the new scored criteria are unknown until the reissued RFP publishes. Shaping those criteria before publication is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Leslie Crow requested Coupa differentiation
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Erica Doucet-Maye on vendor-master compliance and reporting
SF Business_Requirements__c unfilled; RFP 26-03 (SF activities)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a procurement-led competitive RFP with a broad committee. The 21 Oct 2025 demo drew 14 BakerRipley stakeholders spanning procurement (Erica Doucet-Maye, Scarlett Naquin, Melisa Miles), finance (Muhammadou Drammeh, Controller), legal/contracts (Leslie Crow, Matthew Smith, Madonna Long), AP (Neatheas Malveaux) and treasury (Jonathan Morris), showing a genuine cross-functional evaluation. The 17 Mar 2026 'FW: RFP 26-03 Virtual Vendor's Meeting' was a group session where Coupa, Conga and Ivalua were also present. The process then broke: BakerRipley cancelled the RFP on 20 May to redraw scope and, per Erica's 1 Jul reply, will reissue it through the public 'Become a Vendor' portal. No approval chain, shortlist rule or decision date has been confirmed post-cancellation, which is the core process risk.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: 14-stakeholder attendee list
SF event 17 Mar 2026: RFP 26-03 vendor's meeting
Next_Step__c 20 May 2026: RFP cancelled, redrawing scope
SF email 1 Jul 2026: reissue via vendor portal

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown because the deal has not reached commercials. This is new business (iSource, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake) with no prior Closed-Won on the account, so a full MSA plus Order Form plus SOW would eventually be required, but no legal, InfoSec or procurement-onboarding track has opened. On the demo Mike McCarthy pre-empted the paper story by stressing a 'Zycus on Zycus' implementation ('when we get to the statement of work and project review, that also will be Zycus') and prebuilt connectors (DocuSign, D&B, Ecovadis), which pre-answers the SI question but no SOW or redline work exists yet. Legal capacity is present in the room (Leslie Crow, Matthew Smith, Madonna Long), which is a paper-process asset for later. Visibility will come only once the reissued RFP defines the contracting vehicle and timeline.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Mike McCarthy on Zycus-on-Zycus implementation and prebuilt connectors
OpportunityContactRole: Leslie Crow, Matthew Smith, Madonna Long (legal/contracts)
SF What_is_the_decision_process__c / Current_Contract_Expiration__c unfilled

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and operationally specific from the 21 Oct 2025 demo, concentrated in three functions. Procurement runs sourcing manually with no centralized RFI/RFP tooling (Erica Doucet-Maye, Scarlett Naquin). Contracts and legal process vendor compliance (W-9/TIN, SAM/TAG federal checks) by hand and lack automated tax/banking validation (Erica's storage question; Matthew Smith, Contracts Attorney Manager, newly hired and evaluating). CLM pain centers on auto-renewals without utilization tracking (Leslie Crow engaged closely on spend-threshold alerts and CLM). AP triages invoices from an email mailbox manually with downstream exceptions (Neatheas Malveaux, AP Supervisor). The pain is confirmed and cross-functional but unquantified, and its owner is distributed rather than held by a single sponsor, which weakens the compelling event.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Erica Doucet-Maye on manual sourcing and vendor compliance
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Leslie Crow on CLM renewal/spend alerts
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: AP mailbox pain (Neatheas Malveaux)

champion.narrative: There is no true champion. Erica Doucet-Maye (Procurement Specialist) is the most engaged and responsive contact, coordinated the demo, is named in the recent activity trail, and replied on 1 Jul offering to send the reissued RFP, but she acts as a neutral procurement gatekeeper running a public RFP, not a Zycus advocate: she offered no EB introduction, no competitive intel, and no internal push, and the follow-up demo she committed to schedule in Oct 2025 never happened. Melisa Miles (Senior Manager, Purchasing and Vendor Relations) is the senior process owner but has been passive. Building a champion with access to Controller Muhammadou Drammeh, ideally by converting the legal CLM interest (Leslie Crow) or Erica into an internal sponsor, ranks above any commercial step.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Erica Doucet-Maye coordinated, committed to schedule follow-up
SF email 1 Jul 2026: Erica offered reissued RFP via public portal (gatekeeper posture)
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c unfilled

competition.narrative: This is an active competitive RFP, not a sole-source. Coupa is the front-running rival: BakerRipley demoed Coupa before Zycus and Leslie Crow asked us to out-differentiate it on the 21 Oct 2025 call ('we just did a demo of Coupa... if you could highlight any differences... reasons why your system is better than Coupa'). The 17 Mar 2026 vendor's meeting for RFP 26-03 also included Conga (CLM specialist) and Ivalua (source-to-pay suite), so the field is at least four vendors on classical P2P/SIM criteria. Our wedge is a homegrown single-codebase source-to-pay suite with Merlin GenAI and Teams-embedded intake for change management, which Mike McCarthy (ex-Coupa) positioned directly against Coupa's acquired/bolt-on stack. The risk is that the AI wedge is not landing (AIS Negative, Merlin never surfaced from the buyer) and the redrawn RFP could re-anchor scoring on classical P2P where Coupa and Ivalua are strong.
sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Leslie Crow requested Zycus-vs-Coupa differentiation
SF event 17 Mar 2026 + AIS_Why: Coupa, Conga, Ivalua vendor meeting
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Mike McCarthy positioned homegrown vs Coupa

critical_signals.text: BakerRipley ran a formal RFP bake-off on 17 March with Coupa, Conga and Ivalua; Coupa is the front-runner after they demoed it first and asked us to prove we beat it.
BakerRipley cancelled RFP 26-03 on 20 May and is reissuing it with redrawn scope, so we are re-competing from scratch rather than progressing.
The booked amount was cut from 185,000 to 150,000 dollars on 1 May as the scope was redrawn, a defensive cost-cutting signal.
The 21 October demo landed well on homegrown CLM and Merlin AI, but the promised second demo never happened and no working session has occurred in eight months.
Controller Muhammadou Drammeh chaired the demo and is the likely budget owner, but he has stayed passive and no one is sponsoring Zycus internally.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A competitive P2P and contract-management RFP (Zycus versus Coupa, Conga and Ivalua) that BakerRipley cancelled on 20 May and is redrawing; Erica Doucet-Maye confirmed a reissued RFP on 1 Jul, but with no engaged economic buyer, no champion, a Negative AI read and the amount cut to $150k, we are one of four vendors waiting on a restarted process.

win_position.text: We are a credible contender in a live four-vendor RFP: the 21 Oct 2025 demo landed well on homegrown single-codebase CLM, Merlin AI and Teams-embedded intake, but BakerRipley cancelled the RFP on 20 May and is redrawing scope, so no vendor is selected and the field is reset.
Buyer preference is neutral: Erica Doucet-Maye's 1 Jul reply only offered to send the reissued RFP via the public 'Become a Vendor' portal, with no Zycus-specific next step.
Coupa is the front-running rival (BakerRipley demoed it first and asked us to differentiate against it, Leslie Crow 21 Oct 2025), with Conga and Ivalua also in the 17 Mar vendor meeting.

deal_momentum.text: The RFP was cancelled on 20 May, the amount cut from $185k to $150k on 1 May, and the close date has slipped four times from March to 15 Oct 2026.
No buyer call has occurred since the 21 Oct 2025 demo; the 17 Mar vendor's meeting was a group RFP session, not a Zycus working session.
Engagement is not dead: Erica Doucet-Maye replied on 1 Jul confirming a reissued RFP is coming and pointed Bailey Erazo to the BakerRipley vendor portal.

customer_commitment.text: No economic-buyer sponsorship: Controller Muhammadou Drammeh chaired the demo roll-call but has not engaged commercially since 21 Oct 2025.
No champion is advocating internally; Erica Doucet-Maye is a procurement gatekeeper running a public RFP, not a Zycus sponsor, and the follow-up demo she promised in Oct 2025 never happened.
Fourteen BakerRipley stakeholders across procurement, legal, contracts, AP and treasury attended the demo, so the account is broadly mapped even if none is bought-in.

deal_risk.text: Scope reset: the RFP was cancelled on 20 May and is being redrawn ('going back to the drawing board'), so we re-compete from scratch against Coupa, Conga and Ivalua.
AIS is Negative (4.0): Merlin and intake AI never surfaced from the buyer on the demo, so our AI wedge is not landing and the redrawn RFP may re-anchor on classical P2P.
Procurement-driven delay is the main threat: BakerRipley controls the timeline through a public RFP and has repeatedly slipped it, a no-decision risk more than a loss to one rival.

customer_preference.evidence: 1 Jul reply offered only a public-portal RFP link, no Zycus-specific lean (Erica Doucet-Maye)

deal_momentum.evidence: RFP cancelled 20 May, amount cut, close date slipped 4x, no call in 8 months

champion.evidence: No advocate; Erica is a neutral procurement gatekeeper, EB Drammeh passive

deal_risk.evidence: Scope reset plus 4-vendor bake-off plus Negative AIS

scope_change.detail: Amount cut from $185k to $150k on 1 May 2026, then the RFP was cancelled on 20 May and BakerRipley went 'back to the drawing board'. The 20 May note says scope is being changed 'adding more functionality', yet the booked amount was cut, a defensive cost-cutting and phased-buying signal rather than an expansion.

deal_movement.summary: Verdict is improving from prior Off Track to Slowing: after the RFP was cancelled 20 May and the buyer went quiet, Erica Doucet-Maye re-engaged on 1 Jul confirming a reissued RFP is coming. Momentum remains thin, the process has restarted from scratch, and the single most likely blocker to close is a procurement-driven, no-decision delay in republishing the RFP.

competitive_position.summary: Active four-vendor RFP, recency-weighted. Coupa is the strongest current threat: BakerRipley demoed it before Zycus and asked us to out-differentiate it (Leslie Crow, 21 Oct 2025), and it was present at the 17 Mar 2026 vendor's meeting where Coupa displacement/comparison was the dominant theme. Conga (CLM specialist) and Ivalua (source-to-pay suite) are the other named finalists from that 17 Mar meeting, each a medium threat on classical P2P/SIM criteria. Our wedge is a homegrown single-codebase suite with Merlin GenAI and Teams-embedded intake versus Coupa's acquired stack, but the AI wedge is not landing (AIS Negative) and the redrawn RFP could re-anchor scoring on classical P2P where Coupa and Ivalua are strong.

competitors.how_we_win: Homegrown single-codebase CLM and Merlin AI depth versus Coupa's acquired bolt-ons; Teams-embedded intake for adoption; Mike McCarthy's ex-Coupa credibility on the Pepsi-challenge AI comparison.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit demonstrated on the 21 Oct 2025 demo across sourcing, SIM, CLM and P2P, with clear alignment on Teams-embedded intake, single-codebase architecture and Zycus-on-Zycus implementation; exposure is on the Coupa comparison, the unquantified AI value, and the P2P-invoice/reporting depth the buyer still wants to see.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for an early-stage, procurement-driven RFP that has just reset.
flags: Get ahead of the reissued RFP: shape the new scope with Erica Doucet-Maye and legal before it publishes so our CLM and Teams-intake strengths become scored criteria.
Convert Controller Muhammadou Drammeh from demo attendee to sponsor; no economic buyer is bought in and procurement is running a neutral public RFP.
Fix the AI read: Merlin and intake never surfaced from the buyer, so put a focused Merlin CLM and Teams-intake proof in front of Leslie Crow, who owns the CLM interest.

champion_strength.summary: No true champion. Erica Doucet-Maye (Procurement Specialist) is the most engaged and responsive contact and re-confirmed the reissued RFP on 1 Jul, but she acts as a neutral procurement gatekeeper rather than a Zycus advocate: no EB introduction, no competitive intel, and the follow-up demo she committed to in Oct 2025 never happened. Melisa Miles is the senior process owner but passive, and Leslie Crow (legal) shows the strongest product interest and is the best champion-build target. Securing an internal advocate with access to Controller Drammeh is the top qualification move.

The AI story is being pushed by Zycus, not pulled by BakerRipley.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $185k to $150k on 1 May 2026, then the RFP was cancelled on 20 May and BakerRipley went 'back to the drawing board'. The 20 May note says scope  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 18 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 23 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $185k to $150k on 1 May 2026, then the RFP was cancelled on 20 May and BakerRipley went 'back to….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 18 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 8 is anchored on the stage win (23), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: No economic-buyer sponsorship; Controller Muhammadou Drammeh chaired the demo roll-call but has not engaged commercially since 21 Oct 2025
No champion is advocating internally; Erica Doucet-Maye is a procurement gatekeeper running a public RFP, not a Zycus sponsor, and the follow-up demo she promised in Oct 2025 never happened
Fourteen BakerRipley stakeholders across procurement, legal, contracts, AP and treasury attended the demo, so the account is broadly mapped even if none is bought-in

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Confirm the reissued RFP scope, criteria and timeline directly with Erica Doucet-Maye. (by 13 Jul)
Book a scoping call with Controller Muhammadou Drammeh to align the redrawn scope to budget. (by 20 Jul)
Send Erica the Zycus-versus-Coupa CLM and AI differentiator materials mapped to RFP 26-03. (by 17 Jul)

customer_preference.evidence: Erica Doucet-Maye's 1 Jul reply offered only to send the reissued RFP via the public 'Become a Vendor' portal, a neutral 'just send us info' signal with no Zycus-specific next step.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative operational pain across procurement, contracts, CLM and AP articulated on 21 Oct 2025, but no ROI, savings, cycle-time or FTE numbers stated by the buyer; the only figure (6-8% tail-spend savings) was rep-stated by Devika, not buyer-validated.

calls_omitted.reason: Group vendor meeting, not recorded/transcribed in Avoma (SF Clari event only); Coupa, Conga and Ivalua also present per AIS_Why

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Q1/Q2/Q3 opportunity fields + field history (365d) + tasks/events (120d) + OpportunityContactRole
gaps: Only one buyer call (21 Oct 2025 demo) transcribed; no call in 8+ months
Redrawn RFP scope, criteria and decision timeline unknown post-20 May cancellation
Economic-buyer budget authority (Controller Drammeh) inferred, not confirmed

validation gate: an active competitor is named in no Salesforce competition field (Competitors__c / Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c / Next_Step__c) and in no verbatim buyer quote — it is unanchored and was likely inferred to fill a shortlist (offending: 'Conga')

validation gate: an active competitor is named in no Salesforce competition field (Competitors__c / Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c / Next_Step__c) and in no verbatim buyer quote — it is unanchored and was likely inferred to fill a shortlist (offending: 'Ivalua')
